pipeline_preferences.description: Studio pipeline defaults. Material binding: prefer the materiallinker LOP over assignmaterial in Solaris (collection-based binding). Karma CPU production preset for diffuse-dominant hero surfaces: bucket mode, 256 pixel / 512 path-traced samples, indirect guiding on, variance AA 0.005. AMD asset library path is pending capture.
pipeline_preferences.summary: Pipeline preferences: materiallinker over assignmaterial; Karma CPU preset

camera_setup.description: Camera LOP creates a camera prim at /cameras/node_name. Key parms: 'tx/ty/tz' (position), 'rx/ry/rz' (rotation), 'focalLength' (focal length in mm, default 50), 'fStop' (aperture for DOF), 'focusDistance' (focus distance), 'horizontalAperture' (sensor width in mm). For render, camera is referenced by USD prim path (e.g. '/cameras/render_cam'), not Houdini node path. Typical setup: camera at (4,3,6), rotation (-20,30,0) for 3/4 view.
camera_setup.summary: Camera creation and configuration in Solaris

camera_workflow_reference.description: Comprehensive camera workflow reference. Camera types: standard perspective, orthographic, stereo rig. Essential parameters: focalLength (mm), horizontalAperture (36=full frame), verticalAperture, fStop (0=no DOF), focusDistance, clippingRange. Focal length cheat sheet: 14-24mm ultra-wide (environments), 24-35mm wide (interiors), 35-50mm normal (general), 50-85mm portrait (characters), 85-135mm telephoto (close-ups), 135-200mm+ long (compression). DOF setup: fStop non-zero, focusDistance to subject. Quick DOF reference: product (2.0-4.0), portrait (2.8-5.6), landscape (8.0-16.0), miniature (1.4-2.0). Motion blur: transform blur (xformsamples=2), deformation blur (geosamples=2, geovelblur=1). Shutter settings: forward (0.0-1.0), centered (-0.5-0.5), backward (-1.0-0.0). Camera animation: keyframing via Edit LOP, turntable orbit pattern (radius/height/frames), look-at constraint. Multi-camera workflows: render_cam, turntable_cam, closeup_cam, wide_cam. Batch rendering via TOPS/PDG. Composition: rule of thirds, head room, lead room, framing depth.
camera_workflow_reference.summary: Camera workflows: types, focal length, DOF, motion blur, animation, turntable, multi-camera, composition guidelines

chops.description: Channel Operators (CHOPs) for audio processing, motion analysis and retargeting, keyframe/channel manipulation, CHOP wrangles with VEX, and workflows for piping channel data into SOPs, DOPs, and LOPs.
chops.summary: Houdini CHOPs (Channel Operators): audio workflows, motion data, channel manipulation, wrangles, and SOP/DOP/LOP integration.

common_attributes.description: Key geometry attributes: P (position vec3), N (normal vec3), Cd (color vec3), v (velocity vec3), pscale (float), orient (quaternion vec4), up (vec3), id (int), age/life (float), rest (vec3), uv (vec3), width (curve width float). Copy-to-points reads: P, orient (highest priority), N+up, pscale, scale. Pyro volumes: density, temperature, flame, fuel, vel. FLIP: viscosity, surface, vorticity. RBD: constraint_name, strength. Intrinsics: measuredarea, measuredperimeter, measuredvolume.
common_attributes.summary: Standard Houdini geometry attributes and their types

common_errors.description: Node/path errors: matlib cook error (cook first), node not found (check context), node type not found (check spelling). Parameter errors: parm not found on USD (use encoded names), keyframe won't set (delete expression first), parm tuple mismatch (use parmTuple). Render errors: black render (camera prim path), fireflies (intensity>1 violation, roughness=0), slow render (too many bounces). VEX errors: syntax error (semicolons), read-only attribute, wrong run-over mode, pcfind empty. Simulation errors: explosion on frame 1, pyro container clips, drift/instability. File errors: cache missing frames ($F4 padding), USD reference not found.
common_errors.summary: 24 common Houdini errors with causes and solutions

cops_compositing.description: COPs at /img or inside cop2net. Generators: file, color, ramp, noise, font. Color correction: colorcorrect (gamma/gain/offset), grade (lift/gamma/gain), bright, hueshift, lut, tonemap. Compositing: over, multiply, add, screen, blend. Transform: xform, crop, scale, flip. Filters: blur, defocus, sharpen, edge, median. Channels: channelcopy, shuffle, premultiply. Output: rop_comp. Chain: file->colorcorrect->grade->null for basic correction.

copernicus_opencl_reference.description: Complete OpenCL kernel reference for Houdini 21 Copernicus. #bind syntax for layers (src?, !dst, mask?, feedback?), parameters (float/int/float2/float3/float4), ramps (float/float4), volumes, VDBs, geometry attributes. @KERNEL macro, resolution variables (@xres/@yres/@ixres/@iyres), position variables (@ix/@iy/@x/@y). Kernel patterns: color correction (brightness/contrast/saturation with rec709 luminance), Sobel edge detection (3x3), Jump Flooding Algorithm for SDF generation, GPU-friendly hash noise + FBM, Gaussian blur (separable), threshold with smooth falloff, 2D rotation transform. Border modes: constant/clamp/reflect/wrap. Performance: native_sin/cos/sqrt (2x faster), minimize global memory reads, avoid divergent branching (use mix/smoothstep). Debugging: visual debug output, grid overlay, heatmap visualization. Common errors: compilation failed, all-black output, wrong resolution, NaN artifacts.
copernicus_opencl_reference.summary: OpenCL kernel reference for Copernicus: #bind syntax, built-in variables, kernel patterns, performance optimization, debugging

copernicus_solver_patterns.description: Block Begin/End solver architecture for Copernicus feedback loops. Wiring convention: source->Block Begin->processing->Block End->output with auto-feedback port. Invoke COP for external control. Multi-field solvers via cables. Growth propagation (DLA): seed mask + direction field + stochastic expansion kernel, parameter guide (speed/branching/randomness/direction bias), animation strategies (frame sim, static+animated params, growth+dissolve). Reaction-diffusion (Gray-Scott model): feed rate (f=0.02-0.08), kill rate (k=0.04-0.07), diffusion rates (dA=1.0, dB=0.5), pattern map (spots/stripes/labyrinthine/coral). Flow Block 2D fluid: auto-feedback (color/velocity/temperature), force nodes (buoyancy/vorticity/turbulence/axis/custom), license requirement (FX/Indie only). Caching: Simulate ON for scrubbing, Live for exploration. Memory: 3 fields at 4K = 768MB/frame.
copernicus_solver_patterns.summary: Copernicus solver patterns: Block Begin/End, growth propagation, reaction-diffusion, Flow Block 2D fluid, caching, memory management

copernicus_interop_pipelines.description: Cross-context interop workflows for Copernicus. SOP->COP: SOP Import COP (attribute->layer mapping: P/N/Cd/uv/density), Prepare+Rasterize pattern for clean bakes, Volume/VDB import via Geometry to Layer COP, Invoke SOP Block for SOP algorithms on image data. COP->MaterialX: op: path system (file: op:/stage/copnet1/OUT_albedo) for live texture connection, full procedural texture pipeline (noise->albedo/roughness/normal), animated textures (R-D->displacement, growth->roughness). Wetmap workflow: SOP simulation->VDB->COP import->wetness mask->MaterialX mix. Karma AOV compositing: beauty rebuild from light passes (diffuse_direct+indirect, spec_direct+indirect, emission). Slap comp: live viewport compositing in H21. UDIM support: per-tile procedural textures. PDG->COP batch: ROP COP Output TOP, wedged texture generation.
copernicus_interop_pipelines.summary: Copernicus interop: SOP/COP/MaterialX/Karma/PDG cross-context workflows, op: path textures, AOV compositing, wetmaps, batch processing

copernicus_motion_stylization.description: Motion design and stylization patterns for Copernicus. Pixel sorting: threshold->span detection->sort within spans->iterate, GPU parallel across rows, control (threshold/direction/sort_channel/iterations/mask). Risograph/print: color separation->dither->ink simulation->paper composite, authentic ink colors (Pink/Red/Blue/Green/Yellow/Black sRGB), dither modes (blue noise/halftone/error diffusion), subtractive ink mixing. NPR/toon shading: post-render toon via depth+normal edge detection, color quantization, H21 hatching nodes. Directional occlusion: hemisphere ray marching on height/depth maps for cavity/wear. Stamp texturing: Stamp COP with scattered geometry (P/scale/orient/Cd/spriterot attributes). Frame blend: weighted average (motion blur), maximum hold (light trails), feedback blend (solver). Animation patterns: time access in OpenCL via parameter expressions ($T/$FF).
copernicus_motion_stylization.summary: Copernicus motion design: pixel sorting, risograph, NPR/toon, directional AO, stamp texturing, frame blend, animation patterns

copernicus_python_api.description: Python API for Copernicus COP networks. Creating networks: copnet at /obj (standalone), inside /stage (MaterialX op: path), inside SOP (texture baking). Node types: file, fractal_noise, constant, ramp, color_correct, blur, sharpen, over/add/multiply, xform/crop, opencl, rop_comp. Wiring: setInput(index, source), setDisplayFlag/setRenderFlag. OpenCL node configuration: parm('kernelcode').set(code), access generated parameters. Solver creation: block_begin/block_end pair, method='feedback', iterations, simulate mode. Full procedural texture pipeline: noise->albedo/roughness/normal branches, op: path references. Height-to-normal kernel. Cooking: node.cook(force=True), xRes()/yRes(), allPixels(plane), rop_comp.render(). Network layout: layoutChildren(), setPosition(), setColor(), sticky notes. SYNAPSE integration pattern for execute_python handler.
copernicus_python_api.summary: Copernicus Python API: network/node creation, OpenCL configuration, solver setup, cooking, exporting, SYNAPSE integration

copernicus_performance.description: Copernicus GPU performance and memory reference. RTX 4090 benchmarks: simple color <1ms at 4K, blur 12ms, edge detect 4ms, growth 70ms, R-D 45ms, pixel sort 120ms, full solver 150-800ms. GPU 10-100x faster than VEX CPU. Memory estimation: Mono 4K=64MB, RGBA 4K=256MB per layer. Solver cache: 3 fields x 4K x 100 frames = ~9.5GB. Optimization: native_sin/cos/sqrt/exp (2x faster), minimize global memory reads (read once into float4 local), avoid divergent branching (use mix/smoothstep), separable filters (10x fewer samples), resolution strategy (develop 1K, test 2K, final 4K). Kernel complexity tiers: memory-bound (<2ms), balanced (<15ms), compute-bound (<100ms), iterative (<200ms). Work group: let Houdini auto-schedule (RTX 4090: 128 SMs, warp 32). Profiling via Python time.perf_counter(). Common pitfalls: redundant reads, standard vs native math, non-separable filters, too many iterations, RGBA when Mono suffices.
copernicus_performance.summary: Copernicus GPU performance: RTX 4090 benchmarks, memory estimation, optimization patterns, profiling, resolution strategy

crowds.description: Complete crowd simulation pipeline including Agent SOP setup, Crowd Source, DOP network with Crowd Solver, state machines with triggers and transitions, terrain adaptation and foot locking, agent shape/clip variations, and Karma/Solaris rendering workflows.
crowds.summary: Houdini Crowd simulation: agent setup, crowd source, DOP solver, states/triggers/transitions, terrain following, agent variations, and rendering.

flip_simulation.description: FLIP fluids use the flipsolver SOP. Chain: source geometry -> fluidsource -> flipsolver -> particlefluidsurface (meshing). Key parms: 'particlesep' (particle separation, 0.1 preview / 0.03-0.05 final), 'gridscale' (2.0 default), 'viscosity' (0=water, 50+=honey), 'surfacetension'. Velocity transfer: FLIP (splashy), PIC (smooth), blend (picflip=0.05 for best results). Enable 'narrowband' for open-surface sims (5-10x faster). VDB collisions preferred. Whitewater: flipsolver -> whitewatersource -> whitewatersolver for spray/foam/bubbles.
flip_simulation.summary: FLIP fluid simulation setup in SOPs

hapi.description: HAPI is the C API for embedding Houdini in external applications (game engines, DCC tools). Session management: HAPI_Initialize (call once before any HAPI functions, takes cook_options, threading config, search paths), HAPI_Cleanup (frees all resources), HAPI_IsInitialized. Asset loading: HAPI_LoadAssetLibraryFromFile (loads HDA, returns library_id), HAPI_GetAvailableAssetCount, HAPI_CreateNode (creates node under parent, optional cook_on_creation). Geometry access: HAPI_GetGeoInfo, HAPI_GetPartInfo, HAPI_GetAttributeInfo (takes owner: VERTEX/POINT/PRIM/DETAIL), HAPI_GetAttributeFloatData / HAPI_SetAttributeFloatData (strided array access), HAPI_CommitGeo (must call after setting attributes). Cooking: HAPI_CookNode, HAPI_GetStatus (CALL_RESULT/COOK_RESULT/COOK_STATE). Storage types: INT, INT64, FLOAT, FLOAT64, STRING.
hapi.summary: HAPI (Houdini Engine API) C functions for session management, asset loading, and geometry access

hdk.description: HDK is the C++ SDK for extending Houdini with custom nodes and operators. Core classes: GU_Detail (main geometry container -- appendPoint, setPos3, GU_PrimPoly::build, addFloatTuple for attributes), GA_Attribute (attribute storage via GA_RWHandleF/GA_ROHandleV3, iteration with GA_FOR_ALL_PTOFF), SOP_Node (custom SOP base class -- myConstructor, myTemplateList, cookMySop override), PRM_Template (parameter definition with PRM_Name, PRM_Default, types like PRM_FLT/PRM_XYZ). Compiled SOP blocks: setIsThreadSafe(true) on OP_Operator registration. Thread-safe cooking: UT_AutoInterrupt for cancellation. Build system: CMake with find_package(Houdini REQUIRED), target_link_libraries(Houdini), houdini_configure_target().
hdk.summary: HDK (Houdini Development Kit) C++ API for custom SOP nodes, geometry manipulation, and parameter templates

houdini_expressions.description: Frame variables: $F (int), $FF (float), $T (time), $FSTART/$FEND, $FPS. Geometry: $PT, $NPT, $PR, $NPR, $CEX/Y/Z, $SIZEX/Y/Z. Channels: ch('parm'), chs('string'), chv('vector'), chramp('ramp',pos). Inputs: opinput('.', 0), opinputpath. Strings: $HIP, $HIPNAME, $OS, $JOB. File padding: $F4 = 4-digit frame. HScript vs Python vs VEX: HScript for parm fields, Python for complex expressions, VEX in wrangles. Gotcha: ch() in parm fields (HScript) vs ch() in wrangles (VEX) are different.
houdini_expressions.summary: HScript expressions, variables, and channel references

houdini_sop_basics.description: SOP sphere: 'radx/rady/radz' (not 'rad'), 'scale', 'rows', 'cols'. SOP box: 'sizex/y/z', 'tx/ty/tz'. scatter: 'npts'. attribwrangle: 'snippet', 'class' (point/prim/vertex/detail). copytopoints: reads @P, @orient, @N+@up, @pscale, @scale from target points. Groups: groupcreate, groupexpression, grouprange. VDB workflow: mesh -> vdbfrompolygons -> vdbcombine -> convertVDB (reliable booleans). For-each: foreach_begin/end (by pieces, count, feedback). SOP vs LOP parm names differ.
houdini_sop_basics.summary: Common SOP node types, groups, VDB workflow, and parameter names

hydra_delegates.description: Hydra is Pixar's rendering framework in USD. Architecture: USD Stage -> UsdImagingDelegate -> HdRenderIndex -> HdRenderDelegate (your renderer) with HdRenderPass, HdRenderBuffer, and Rprim/Sprim/Bprim scene primitives. Core classes: HdRendererPlugin (entry point, discovered via plugInfo.json with bases=['HdRendererPlugin']), HdRenderDelegate (renderer core -- GetSupportedRprimTypes, CreateRprim/Sprim/Bprim, CreateRenderPass, GetRenderSettingDescriptors), HdRenderPass (_Execute called per frame), HdRenderBuffer (Allocate, Map/Unmap, Resolve). Prim categories: Rprim (Mesh, BasisCurves, Points, Volume), Sprim (Camera, Light, Material), Bprim (RenderBuffer). Karma pattern: hdKarma (delegate DLL) + usdKarma (schema resource). Lifecycle: Houdini start -> PXR_PLUGINPATH scan -> plugInfo discovery -> user selects renderer -> CreateRenderDelegate -> per-frame _Execute -> DeleteRenderDelegate.
hydra_delegates.summary: Hydra render delegate architecture: HdRenderDelegate, plugInfo.json, Karma dual-plugin pattern

joy_of_vex_deformation.description: Tutorial examples covering wave displacement and ramp-driven deformation from The Joy of VEX. Covers functions: @Cd, @Frame, @N, @P, @Py, @Time, @offset, @primnum, @ptnum, @scale, @up, abs, ch, chf, chi, chramp, cos, distance, fit, fit01.

joy_of_vex_geometry_creation.description: Tutorial examples covering addpoint, addprim, and procedural geometry from The Joy of VEX. Covers functions: @Frame, @N, @OpInput1, @P, @Time, @Tramp, @i, @id, @noise, @nrelpe, @opinput1, @primnum, @ptnum, @time, addpoint, addprim, addvertex, ch, ch3, chf.

joy_of_vex_noise.description: Tutorial examples covering snoise, curlnoise, and random patterns from The Joy of VEX. Covers functions: $F, $PI, @0, @Frame, @N, @P, @Time, @a, @orient, @primnum, @primuv, @ptnum, @t, @up, @w, PI, addpoint, addprim, ch, chf.

joy_of_vex_pcopen.description: Tutorial examples covering pcopen, pcfilter, and point cloud iteration from The Joy of VEX. Covers functions: @Cd, @N, @P, @dist, @ez, @n, @ptnum, @v, ch, chf, chi, chramp, chv, chvlen, distance, dot, fit, fit01, foreach, i@numpt.

joy_of_vex_quaternions.description: Tutorial examples covering quaternion creation, slerp, qrotate, and orient from The Joy of VEX. Covers functions: $F, $PI, @0, @Cd, @D, @Frame, @N, @O, @P, @PI, @R, @Time, @Time01, @TimeInc, @X, @Z, @a, @chs, @orient, @ptnum.

joy_of_vex_trigonometry.description: Tutorial examples covering sine waves, circular motion, and oscillation from The Joy of VEX. Covers functions: @Cd, @Frame, @N, @P, @Time, @pscale, @ptnum, @scale, @up, CH, asin, ch, chf, chi, chramp, chv, clamp, cos, distance, f@offset.

joy_of_vex_vector_math.description: Tutorial examples covering dot, cross, fit, clamp, and matrix operations from The Joy of VEX. Covers functions: @Cd, @N, @P, @numpt, @ptnum, @up, @v, ch, chramp, chv, clamp, cross, dot, f[]@, fit, float, ident, length, matrix, matrix3.

karma_aov.description: Karma AOVs via karmarenderproperties Image Output section. Common AOVs: beauty (C), direct/indirect diffuse/specular, albedo, N (normals), P (position), Z (depth), motionvector, crypto_material/object/asset (Cryptomatte). Denoising AOVs: denoise_albedo, denoise_normal. Light groups: tag lights with 'lightGroup' for per-light AOVs. Deep output: .exr with deep flag for depth-correct compositing. AOV rebuild: beauty = direct_diffuse + indirect_diffuse + direct_specular + indirect_specular + emission + sss.
karma_aov.summary: Karma AOV setup: render passes, cryptomatte, denoising

karma_render_reference.description: Full Karma rendering reference. Engine comparison: XPU (GPU+CPU hybrid, 5-20x faster) vs CPU (reference quality, OSL support, nested dielectrics). Render settings: pixel samples (karma:global:pathtracedsamples), convergence mode, pixel oracle. Bounce limits: diffuse (diffuselimit), specular (reflectlimit), transmission (refractlimit), volume (volumelimit), SSS (ssslimit). Progressive render pipeline: Stage 1 layout (320x240, 4 samples), Stage 2 lighting (960x540, 16-32), Stage 3 quality (1920x1080, 64), Stage 4 final (128-512 samples). Intel OIDN denoising (denoisemode='oidn'). 9 beauty AOVs (C, direct/indirect diffuse/specular, emission, sss, direct/indirect volume) + 8 utility AOVs (N, P, depth, Albedo, motionvector, crypto_material/object/asset). Volume rendering: step rate, shadow step rate. Camera DOF (fStop > 0), motion blur (xformsamples, geosamples). Light linking via USD collections. Light groups for per-light AOVs. 8 common render issues with fixes.
karma_render_reference.summary: Comprehensive Karma rendering: XPU vs CPU, render settings, progressive pipeline, denoising, AOVs, volumes, camera DOF, light linking

karma_rendering.description: Karma render types in /out: 'karma' (direct driver with 'picture' and 'camera' parms, 'engine' parm for xpu/cpu), 'usdrender' (USD-based with 'loppath', 'outputimage', 'override_camera', 'override_res' parms). The 'override_res' parm is a string menu with values '', 'scale', 'specific'. Resolution parms: 'res_user1' (width) and 'res_user2' (height) on usdrender, 'resolutionx'/'resolutiony' on karma. Camera must be USD prim path (e.g. '/cameras/render_cam'), not Houdini node path. ROP in /out needs 'loppath' set to a LOP display node. Karma XPU has ~10-15s file flush delay after render() returns.
karma_rendering.summary: Karma render engine configuration and ROP setup

kinefx_rigging.description: KineFX operates in SOPs: skeleton = points (joints) + polylines (bones) + @name + @transform attributes. Creation: skeleton SOP or kinefx_characterunpack (from FBX/USD). Retargeting: mappoints -> retargetbiped transfers animation between different skeletons. Full-body IK: rigfullbodyik with joint targets. Motion clips: motionclip (create), motionclipblend (crossfade), motionclipsequence (chain clips). Skinning: bonecapture (compute weights) -> bonedeform (apply animation to mesh). VEX works directly on skeletons since they're just points.
kinefx_rigging.summary: KineFX SOP-based rigging, retargeting, and motion clips

lighting_setup.description: Solaris light types: domelight (environment/HDRI), distantlight (sun/directional), rectlight (area light), disklight (disk area), cylinderlight (tube light), spherelight (point light). LIGHTING LAW: intensity ALWAYS 1.0, brightness controlled by EXPOSURE (logarithmic, in stops). Encoded parm names: intensity='xn__inputsintensity_i0a' (always 1.0), color='xn__inputscolor_zta' (vec3), exposure='xn__inputsexposure_vya', exposure control='xn__inputsexposure_control_wcb' (set to 'set' to enable). Dome light texture: 'xn__inputstexturefile_r3ah'. Key:fill ratio math: fill_exposure = key_exposure - log2(ratio). Standard 3-point: key=5.0, fill=3.0 (4:1), rim=4.5, dome=0.0.
lighting_setup.summary: Light types, Lighting Law, and exposure-based brightness control in Solaris

materialx_reference.description: Comprehensive MaterialX shader reference. Standard Surface 11 input groups: Base (base, base_color), Specular (specular, specular_roughness, specular_IOR), Metalness, Transmission, SSS (subsurface, subsurface_color/radius/scale), Emission, Coat, Sheen, Thin Film, Normal, Opacity. 21 encoded parameter names (basecolor, metalness, specular_roughness, specular_IOR, coat, coat_roughness, emission, emission_color, etc.). 10 material presets: brushed metal (metalness=1, roughness=0.25), polished chrome (roughness=0.02), matte plastic (roughness=0.4), glossy plastic (coat=0.5), clear glass (transmission=1), frosted glass (roughness=0.3, transmission=1), skin SSS (subsurface=0.6), marble (subsurface=0.4), fabric/velvet (sheen=0.8), car paint (metalness=0.5, coat=1). PBR texture set: diffuse/albedo (sRGB), roughness/metalness/normal/displacement (Raw/Linear). UDIM tiles (<UDIM> token). Color space rules. Displacement setup (displacement_scale, displacement_offset, displacement bound, subdivision). Material assignment: direct binding, collection-based, GeomSubset. 12 common MaterialX nodes.
materialx_reference.summary: MaterialX Standard Surface: input groups, encoded parameter names, material presets, PBR textures, UDIM, displacement

materialx_shaders.description: MaterialX shaders are created inside a materiallibrary LOP node. Use 'mtlxstandard_surface' type. IMPORTANT: After creating the materiallibrary, call matlib.cook(force=True) before creating child shader nodes — otherwise createNode() returns None. Key parms: 'base_colorr/g/b' (base color RGB, 0-1), 'metalness' (0=dielectric, 1=metal), 'specular_roughness' (0=mirror, 1=rough), 'specular' (specular weight), 'coat' (clearcoat weight), 'emission_colorr/g/b' (emission), 'opacity' (transparency). Assign with 'assignmaterial' LOP: 'primpattern1' for target prim, 'matspecpath1' for material path (e.g. '/materials/my_shader').
materialx_shaders.summary: MaterialX shader creation in Solaris MaterialLibrary

ocean_fx.description: Ocean system: oceanspectrum (wave frequency data) -> oceanevaluate (displaced geometry). For interaction: add oceanflat -> flipsolver for FLIP tank blended with spectral ocean. Key spectrum parms: speed (wind m/s), direction, spread, chop (wave peakiness), gridsize, depth. Stack 2-3 spectrums for realism (swell + chop + ripples). Whitewater: flipsolver -> whitewatersource -> whitewatersolver for spray/foam/bubbles. Emission from cusp (wave crests), velocity, acceleration. Ocean shader: IOR 1.33, very low roughness, deep blue-green absorption color.
ocean_fx.summary: Ocean spectrum, FLIP interaction, and whitewater setup

pipeline_integration.description: Studio pipeline integration patterns. File conventions: $HIP/$JOB paths, _v001 versioning, $F4 frame padding, .bgeo.sc/.vdb/.exr formats. ShotGrid/Ftrack: project/sequence/shot/asset/task/version hierarchy, publishing workflow, status tracking (wtg/ip/rev/apr). Render farm: Deadline (chunk 1 for sims, 5-10 for renders), HQueue, Tractor. ACES color management: ACEScg working space, sRGB input for textures, Raw for data maps, EXR linear output. Asset management: USD references for assets, variant sets for LOD, lowercase_underscores naming. Cache workflow: filecache SOP with $HIP/cache paths.
pipeline_integration.summary: VFX studio pipeline: ShotGrid, file conventions, render farm, ACES, review tools

pxr_plugin_discovery.description: USD plugin discovery in Houdini via PXR_PLUGINPATH_NAME environment variable. Discovery mechanism: PlugRegistry scans each directory, finds plugInfo.json, follows Includes patterns (e.g., '*/resources/' for one-level subdirectory scan -- NOT recursive), registers Types based on bases declarations, lazy-loads libraries on demand. Houdini's built-in paths: $HFS/houdini/dso/usd_plugins/ (Karma, Storm), $HOUDINI_USER_PREF_DIR/dso/usd/ (user plugins). Package system: JSON files in houdini21.0/packages/ that set env vars with method='append'/'prepend'/'set'. Dual-location deployment: source (git-tracked, via prepend) + runtime (dso/usd/, via append). Root field in plugInfo.json: '.' = dir containing plugInfo, '..' = parent dir. Path priority (highest first): PXR_PLUGINPATH_NAME prepend -> append -> $HFS built-in -> user prefs auto-scan. Verification: hython with Plug.Registry().GetAllPlugins().
pxr_plugin_discovery.summary: PXR_PLUGINPATH_NAME, Houdini packages, plugin discovery mechanism, and path priority

pyro_simulation.description: Modern Houdini pyro uses SOP-level pyrosolver. Chain: source geometry -> scatter (5000+ points) -> attribwrangle (set density=1, temperature=2, flame=1, v=upward velocity, pscale=0.05) -> volumerasterizeattributes (set 'attributes' parm to 'density temperature flame') -> pyrosolver. Key solver parms: 'divsize' (voxel size, 0.05 for detail), 'tempcooling' (0.5-0.8), 'dissipation' (0.1-0.2), 'enable_disturbance' (1 for turbulence). Import to Solaris via 'sopimport' LOP. Cook frame-by-frame for simulation (needs sequential frames).

rbd_simulation.description: RBD in modern Houdini: geometry -> rbdmaterialfracture (Voronoi fracture) -> assemble (pack primitives) -> rigidsolver SOP. Constraint types: Glue (break on impact), Hard (unbreakable), Hinge (rotate), Cone Twist (limited rotation), Soft (spring). Setup: rbdconstraintsfromrules -> rbdconstraintproperties -> solver constraint input. For progressive destruction: vary strength by height or group. Impact data: f@impulse for collision force. Secondary effects: debris via POP solver, dust via pyro at impact points.

render_analysis.description: Exposure analysis: check overall brightness, key:fill ratio, highlight clipping, shadow detail. Adjust via exposure stops (never intensity). Noise types: general (increase samples), shadow (increase min samples), specular/fireflies (clamp, check roughness), volume (increase step rate). Lighting check: key light dominant, fill 1.5-3 stops below, rim separates from background. Material check: roughness 0.001 minimum (not 0.0), metalness 0 or 1 (not between), base color appropriate. Iteration: 640x360 at 16 samples for layout, 1280x720 at 64 for lookdev, full res at 256+ for final.
render_analysis.summary: Analyzing render quality: exposure, noise, lighting, materials

scene_assembly.description: Import external USD files using 'reference' LOP (adds content under a prim path, good for assets) or 'sublayer' LOP (merges layer stack, good for environments). Reference vs Payload: references always load, payloads defer loading until requested. Reference LOP parms: 'filepath1', 'primpath', 'reftype' (Reference or Payload). Layer stacking: anim.usd -> layout.usd -> lighting.usd -> final.usd (later sublayers are stronger).
scene_assembly.summary: USD scene assembly via reference, sublayer, and payload

solaris_node_types.description: Common Solaris LOP node types: sphere, cube, camera, domelight, distantlight, rectlight, cylinderlight, disklight, materiallibrary, assignmaterial, edit, merge, reference, sublayer, sopimport, karmarenderproperties, usdrender_rop, null. Note: there is no 'grid' or 'plane' LOP node — use cube with scale.y=0.01 for ground planes.
solaris_node_types.summary: Solaris/LOP node types available in Houdini

solaris_parameter_names.description: Solaris parameter names follow USD naming with namespace encoding. Common gotchas: Light intensity is 'xn__inputsintensity_i0a' (not 'intensity' or 'light_intensity'). Sphere has no 'rows'/'cols' (those are SOP sphere parms). Camera focal length is 'focalLength'. MaterialX base color channels are 'base_colorr', 'base_colorg', 'base_colorb' (not 'base_color_r'). Edit node uses 'primpattern' for prim selection and 'tx','ty','tz','sx','sy','sz' for transforms.

solaris_nodes.description: Python reference for creating and configuring Solaris (LOP) nodes via hou.node('/stage').createNode() + parm().set(). Covers stage management (merge, switch, null, configurestage), geometry import (sopcreate/sopimport), materials (materiallibrary, assignmaterial, materiallinker), all light types (domelight, distantlight, rectlight, disklight, cylinderlight, spherelight), cameras, render settings (karmarenderproperties, usdrender_rop), and instancing nodes — H22 (22.0.368) renamed the `instancer` LOP to `copytopoints` and the `layout` LOP to `paintinstances` (old spellings gone from type lookup; emit canonical names only; `pointinstancer` is a NEW H22 node, not the rename). All LOP nodes live in /stage. Lighting Law: intensity ALWAYS 1.0; brightness via exposure (stops). Light parms use punycode-encoded names (e.g. exposure='xn__inputsexposure_vya'); see solaris_parameters for the encoding table.
solaris_nodes.summary: Solaris/LOP node creation and configuration reference (stage, geometry, materials, lights, cameras, render settings)

solaris_parameters.description: USD/LOP parameters surface under punycode-encoded names (xn__...) that don't match their friendly USD attribute names — discover them via node.parms()/parmTuple() rather than guessing. Verified light encodings (Houdini 21.0.671, live-probed): intensity='xn__inputsintensity_i0a' (always 1.0), exposure='xn__inputsexposure_vya', exposure control='xn__inputsexposure_control_wcb' ('set'), color (color3f parmTuple base)='xn__inputscolor_zta' (components _ztar/_ztag/_ztab), diffuse='xn__inputsdiffuse_8wa', specular='xn__inputsspecular_vya', normalize='xn__inputsnormalize_i0a', colorTemperature='xn__inputscolorTemperature_wcb' (camelCase), enableColorTemperature='xn__inputsenableColorTemperature_omb', texture file='xn__inputstexturefile_r3ah', texture format='xn__inputstextureformat_06ah'. color3f tuples use parmTuple. Camera attrs (focalLength, fStop, focusDistance) are plain camelCase, NOT encoded. MaterialX base color channels are base_colorr/g/b.
solaris_parameters.summary: Solaris/LOP encoded (xn__) parameter names — verified light-param encodings and parmTuple usage

solaris_network_blueprint.description: Canonical node-wiring order for a Solaris (LOP) network: SOPCreate -> MaterialLibrary -> AssignMaterial -> Camera -> Lights -> RenderProperties -> OUTPUT null. Every node lives in /stage (never /obj). Use sopcreate (not sopimport) for new geometry. Wire linearly with node.setInput(0, previous). Composition: later sublayers/merge inputs are stronger (LIVRPS opinion strength); a reference adds asset content under a prim path, a sublayer merges a layer stack onto /World. Lighting Law: intensity ALWAYS 1.0; brightness via exposure only.
solaris_network_blueprint.summary: Canonical Solaris/LOP network wiring order and composition layering

sop_solver_loops.description: Solver SOP creates frame-to-frame feedback: output of frame N becomes input of frame N+1. Inside solver: Prev_Frame node gives last frame, Input_1 gives original input. Must play forward from start frame (no random access). Common patterns: point growth (expand each frame), trail/history (accumulate points), painting (accumulate attribute by proximity), custom particles (emit + gravity + kill). SOP Solver DOP integrates custom SOP logic inside DOP sims. Key rule: multiply velocities by @TimeInc for frame-rate independence.
sop_solver_loops.summary: SOP Solver feedback loops and custom simulation

terrain_heightfields.description: Heightfield system uses 2D volume grids. Chain: heightfield -> heightfield_noise -> heightfield_erode -> heightfield_scatter. Key parms: gridspacing (resolution, 1.0 default), sizex/sizey (terrain dimensions). Erosion types: hydraulic (water carving channels) and thermal (material sliding down slopes). Erosion parms: iterations (50+), rainamount, eroderate, bankangle. Layers: height, mask, bedrock, debris, sediment, water. Use layers to drive material assignment (height=snow, debris=dirt, slope=cliff). Convert to polygon mesh via heightfield_convert for rendering. Scatter vegetation/rocks using heightfield_scatter with density masks.
terrain_heightfields.summary: Terrain creation with heightfields, erosion, and scatter

tops_wedging.description: TOPs (Task Operator Network) enables parameter wedging. Create a topnet at /obj level. Inside: wedge TOP (generates parameter variations) -> ropfetch TOP (renders each variation). Wedge node: 'wedgecount' sets number of variations, 'wedgeattribs' defines which attributes to vary. Types: Range (linear sweep), Random (exploration), Specific Values (A/B). Schedulers: localscheduler, hqueuescheduler, deadline. For GPU renders (Karma XPU), set maxproccount=1. Use waitforall -> imagemagick for contact sheet comparisons.

usd_composition_reference.description: Comprehensive USD stage composition reference. LIVRPS strength ordering: Local > Inherits > VariantSets > References > Payloads > Sublayers. Multi-department layer stacking (shot_final > fx > lighting > anim > layout > asset). Standard prim hierarchy: /World/cameras, /World/lights, /World/geo, /World/materials. 18 common prim types (Xform, Mesh, BasisCurves, Points, DomeLight, DistantLight, RectLight, Camera, Material, etc.). Transform stack: xformOp:translate, xformOp:rotateXYZ, xformOp:scale with right-to-left execution. VariantSets for switchable configurations. PointInstancer for many copies, native instancing for shared subtrees. Visibility (inherited/invisible) and purpose (default/render/proxy/guide). Material binding patterns: direct, collection-based, GeomSubset face-level. 14 Houdini LOP nodes: sopimport, reference, sublayer, edit, material_library, assign_material, light, camera, render_settings, usdrender, prune, configure_layer, graft, collection.
usd_composition_reference.summary: USD composition arcs (LIVRPS), layer stacking, prim hierarchy, transforms, VariantSets, instancing, and material binding

usd_schema_registration.description: Two deployment strategies for USD schemas in Houdini: Resource-only (no compilation, for UI/settings schemas -- deploy generatedSchema.usda + hand-crafted plugInfo.json with LibraryPath='', Type='resource') and Compiled library (full C++ typed accessors + Python bindings via CMake). usdGenSchema code generator: takes schema.usda input with GLOBAL customData (libraryName, libraryPath, libraryPrefix), outputs api.h, tokens, accessor classes, wrappers, generatedSchema.usda, plugInfo.json template. Schema types: IsA (custom prim types, inherits </Typed>), Single-apply API (settings, inherits </APISchemaBase>, ApplyAPI()), Multi-apply API (per-instance data). Key directives: apiSchemaType, apiSchemaCanOnlyApplyTo, displayGroup, displayName. Karma uses resource-only (Type=resource, no SchemasForRenderers in H21). Third-party delegates need SchemasForRenderers mapping in plugInfo.json. Python: rs.ApplyAPI('MyRenderSettingsAPI'), rs.GetAttribute('myrenderer:steps').Get().
usd_schema_registration.summary: USD schema registration: usdGenSchema, resource-only vs compiled, applying custom API schemas

usd_stage_operations.description: USD prims in Solaris follow a hierarchy. Common paths: /hero_sphere (from sphere LOP named 'hero_sphere'), /materials/shader_name (from materiallibrary), /lights/light_name (from light LOPs), /cameras/cam_name (from camera LOPs), /Render (from karmarenderproperties). Transform uses 'xformOp:transform' matrix (not 'xformOp:translate'). For translate/rotate/scale, use an 'edit' LOP node instead of USD attributes directly. Composition strength (LIVRPS): Local > Inherits > VariantSets > References > Payloads > Sublayers. Prim types: Sphere, Cube, Mesh, Xform, Scope, DomeLight, DistantLight, RectLight, Camera.
usd_stage_operations.summary: USD stage operations, composition arcs (LIVRPS), and prim manipulation

uv_workflows.description: UV attribute: v@uv (vector3, typically on vertices for seam support). Creation: uvunwrap (automatic), uvflatten (production, seam-based), uvproject (planar/cylindrical/spherical). Layout: uvlayout packs islands efficiently with padding. UDIM: tiles for multi-texture assets (1001=body, 1002=head, etc). Transfer: attribtransfer or uvtransfer between different topology meshes. Quality check: uvquickshade applies checker pattern (uniform squares = good UVs). Tips: vertex UVs for production, padding 0.005+ for mip bleeding, transfer UVs early before topology changes.
uv_workflows.summary: UV unwrapping, layout, UDIM, and texturing workflows

vellum_simulation.description: Vellum is Houdini's unified SOP-based solver. Chain: source -> vellumconfigure* -> vellumsolver. Types: vellumcloth (fabric), vellumhair (cables/hair), vellumgrains (sand/snow), vellumsoftbody (jelly/rubber), vellumballoon (inflatable). Configure nodes have TWO outputs: geometry (solver input 1) and constraints (solver input 3). Key cloth parms: stretchstiffness (10000), bendstiffness (0.001), thickness (0.01). Draping: vellumdrape pre-settles garments. Collision: vellumcollider for external objects. Pin: i@stopped=1 on constrained points. Substrate guidelines: 5 substeps for cloth, 1-3 for grains.
vellum_simulation.summary: Vellum cloth, hair, grain, and soft body simulation

vex_attributes.description: VEX attribute access patterns. Built-in point attributes: @P (position), @N (normal), @Cd (color), @v (velocity), @pscale, @orient, @id, @age, @life, @rest, @uv, @Alpha, @width. Context variables: @ptnum, @numpt, @primnum, @numprim, @vtxnum, @elemnum. Globals: @Time, @Frame, @TimeInc. Reading: @-shorthand for current element (fastest), point()/prim()/vertex()/detail() for any element/input. Writing: @attr creates if missing, setpointattrib/setprimattrib/setdetailattrib with mode (set/add/min/max/mult). Groups: i@group_name = 1/0, inpointgroup(), inprimgroup(). Intrinsics: primintrinsic(0, 'measuredarea', @primnum). Promotion patterns: point-to-detail via loops, detail-to-point broadcast, nearest-point transfer via nearpoint/pcfind, distance-weighted blend.
vex_attributes.summary: VEX attribute system: built-in attributes, type prefixes, groups, intrinsics, promotion patterns

vex_functions.description: Complete VEX function signatures organized by category. Geometry read: point/prim/vertex/detail, attribsize, hasattrib. Write: setpointattrib, setprimattrib, addattrib. Create/delete: addpoint, addprim, addvertex, removepoint, removeprim. Spatial: nearpoint, nearpoints, pcfind, pcfind_radius, pcopen, pcfilter, intersect, xyzdist. Connectivity: neighbours, pointprims, primpoints. Math: sin/cos, pow, sqrt, clamp, fit, lerp, smooth, chramp. Noise: noise, snoise, xnoise, curlnoise, flownoise, vnoise. Vector/matrix: length, normalize, dot, cross, maketransform, cracktransform, quaternion, qrotate. String: sprintf, split, join, match, re_match. Channel: ch, chf, chi, chs, chv, chramp. Array: len, append, find, sort, sum, avg.
vex_functions.summary: VEX function reference: 150+ signatures across geometry, math, noise, strings, channels, arrays

vex_fundamentals.description: VEX fundamentals for wrangle nodes. Execution contexts: SOP (Attribute Wrangle), DOP (Gas Field Wrangle), CHOP (Channel Wrangle), COP (Pixel Wrangle). Run-over modes: Points (default, @ptnum), Primitives (@primnum), Vertices (@vtxnum), Detail (single pass). Data types: int, float, vector2, vector, vector4, matrix2, matrix3, matrix, string, arrays. Type prefixes for @-binding: f@=float, i@=int, v@=vector, u@=vector2, p@=vector4, s@=string, 2@/3@/4@=matrices. Control flow: if/else, for, foreach, while, ternary. Inputs: 4 geometry inputs accessed by index (0-3). User-defined functions with semicolon type separators. Preprocessor: #include, #define. VEX vs HScript: VEX is compiled/parallel for heavy processing, HScript for simple parameter expressions.
vex_fundamentals.summary: VEX language basics: execution contexts, run-over modes, data types, control flow, VEX vs HScript

vex_patterns.description: Copy-paste VEX patterns. Deformation: sine wave (@P.y += sin()), noise displacement along normal, twist (rotation around axis), taper via chramp. Color: by height (chramp), by curvature (neighbour dot products), random per piece (hsvtorgb). Generation: spiral points (detail wrangle with trig), polyline from points, quad grid from scratch. Grouping: by bounding box, by normal direction (dot threshold), every Nth point. Solver: point advection with gravity/ground collision, attribute decay (frame-rate independent pow). Transfer: proximity blend via pcfind, smooth/relax via neighbour averaging, edge detection via gradient magnitude. fBm noise layering with octaves.
vex_patterns.summary: 15+ VEX code patterns: deformation, color, generation, grouping, solver, transfer

vex_performance.description: VEX performance patterns. Fast: pcfind (KD-tree O(log n)) over nearpoints (O(n)), distance2/length2 (skip sqrt), @-binding (compiled direct access), smooth/lerp for branchless blends, pre-sized arrays via resize(). Slow: string attribute lookups in loops, dynamic array growth, complex branching. Run-over selection: Points for per-point, Detail + explicit loops for small geo with inter-element deps. Compilation: first cook compiles (slow), ch() changes don't trigger recompile. Profile via Performance Monitor. Common bottlenecks: O(n^2) spatial query -> use pcfind, memory spikes -> use pcopen/pcfilter streaming, string lookups in loops -> cache outside.
vex_performance.summary: VEX optimization: fast/slow patterns, spatial queries, memory, profiling, bottleneck checklist

vex_types.description: VEX type system. Scalars: int (32-bit), float (32-bit), string (UTF-8). Vectors: vector2 (2f), vector (3f/xyz), vector4 (4f/quaternion). Matrices: matrix2 (2x2), matrix3 (3x3 rotation/scale), matrix (4x4 full transform). Implicit promotion: int->float->vector2->vector->vector4. Explicit: int(), float(), set(x,y,z). Component access: v.x/.y/.z or v[0]/[1]/[2]. Quaternion ops: quaternion(angle, axis), eulertoquaternion(), qrotate(), slerp(), qmultiply(), qinvert(). Matrix ops: ident(), maketransform(), cracktransform(), invert(), transpose(), lookat(), dihedral(). String conversion: itoa(), atoi(), atof(), sprintf().
vex_types.summary: VEX data types: int, float, vector, matrix, quaternion, string; conversions and operations

wire_dynamics.description: Wire dynamics via Vellum Hair (recommended) or DOP Wire Solver (legacy). Chain: curve_geo -> vellumhair -> vellumsolver. Key parms: stretchstiffness (50000 for cable), bendstiffness (1.0 for cable, 0.001 for fishing line), density. Presets: thin cable (stretch=50000, bend=1.0), heavy rope (stretch=100000, bend=10.0), power line (stretch=200000, bend=0.01). Pin endpoints: i@stopped=1. Catenary curve: lerp + parabolic sag approximation. Rendering: @width attribute for curve thickness, material per wire type. Wind: vellumconstraintproperty with Wind type or custom VEX force.
wire_dynamics.summary: Wire, cable, rope, and strand dynamics simulation

rag_material_binding_strength.description: Material binding strength override in Solaris. Covers Assign Material LOP bindingstrength parameter, weakerThanDescendants vs strongerThanDescendants, when materials don't apply due to existing bindings, Unassign Material for clearing bindings.
rag_material_binding_strength.summary: Material binding strength and override patterns in Solaris LOPs

rag_material_library_setup.description: Creating materials inside Material Library LOP. CRITICAL: matlib.cook(force=True) BEFORE createNode() on shader children. Multiple material subnets, geopath assignment directly in matlib, wiring order.
rag_material_library_setup.summary: Material Library LOP creation, cooking requirement, and multi-material setup

rag_material_vexpression.description: Dynamic material assignment using VEXpressions in LOPs. CRITICAL: LOPs use @elemnum not @ptnum. Conditional material assignment, attribute-driven assignment via primvars.
rag_material_vexpression.summary: VEXpression-based dynamic material assignment in LOPs (@elemnum, not @ptnum)

rag_material_mtlx_encoding.description: MaterialX as USD prims workflow. How MaterialX nodes become USD shader prims, mtlx: namespace, reading/writing MaterialX parameters through USD API, Sdf.ValueTypeNames, the _shader suffix issue.
rag_material_mtlx_encoding.summary: MaterialX shader encoding as USD prims with Sdf value types

rag_material_primvar_driven.description: shop_materialpath and primvar-driven material assignment. SOP-level attribute propagation to LOPs, primvar readers in MaterialX, using geometry attributes to drive shader parameters.
rag_material_primvar_driven.summary: SOP-to-LOP material assignment via shop_materialpath and primvar-driven shading

rag_material_karma_xpu.description: Karma XPU material constraints. XPU supports MaterialX ONLY (no VEX shaders), checking renderer compatibility, XPU-unsupported features (custom AOVs, some SSS modes), fallback to CPU.
rag_material_karma_xpu.summary: Karma XPU material constraints — MaterialX only, no VEX shaders

rag_material_path_expressions.description: USD path expressions vs Houdini prim patterns for material assignment. Exact prim paths required for bindings, wildcard patterns for targeting, collection-based targeting.
rag_material_path_expressions.summary: USD path expressions vs Houdini glob patterns — exact paths required for material bindings

rag_material_collect_vop.description: Multi-renderer material switching. Collect VOP pattern for multi-output materials across Karma CPU, XPU, and other delegates. USD material purpose outputs.
rag_material_collect_vop.summary: Multi-renderer material switching with Collect VOP and USD material outputs

rag_material_texture_override.description: Texture parameter overrides in Karma. Per-prim texture variation via Edit Properties LOP, UDIM setup, CPU vs XPU texture handling differences.
rag_material_texture_override.summary: Texture overrides, UDIM setup, and CPU vs XPU texture handling

rag_material_common_errors.description: Consolidated material error patterns. Grey/default shader (binding didn't take), material not visible (check binding strength), matlib cook error, wrong prim path, @ptnum vs @elemnum, scope mismatch.
rag_material_common_errors.summary: Material error diagnostics: grey shader, invisible material, matlib cook, path mismatch

rag_dop_wiring_order.description: DOP node wiring conventions. DOPs use merge-based connections (NOT SOP-style direct wiring). Objects first, then forces, then solvers via merge nodes. enforceWiringOrder, findOrCreateMergeInput pattern.
rag_dop_wiring_order.summary: DOP merge-based wiring conventions: objects → forces → solver via merge

rag_dop_simulation_guard.description: ALWAYS wrap DOP network creation/modification in simulation-enabled guard. hou.setSimulationEnabled(False) try/finally pattern prevents crashes and incorrect initial state.
rag_dop_simulation_guard.summary: Simulation-enabled guard pattern for safe DOP network editing

rag_dop_solver_creation.summary: DOP solver creation: Bullet, Pyro, FLIP, Vellum with substep guidelines

rag_dop_data_tree.description: DopData hierarchy navigation. Simulation → objects → subdata (geometry, forces, solver). freeze() for extracting geometry, DOP records for metadata, recursive subdata listing.
rag_dop_data_tree.summary: DOP data tree navigation, freeze() pattern, and record access

rag_dop_rbd_setup.description: Complete RBD simulation setup via Python. Packed Object workflow, Bullet solver, constraint networks, collision geometry, activation/deactivation, ground plane.
rag_dop_rbd_setup.summary: RBD packed object setup: fracture, constraints, Bullet solver, collision

rag_dop_checkpoint.description: DOP checkpoint and recovery. .sim file caching, checkpoint intervals, resume from checkpoints, File Cache SOP for geometry output, disk space management.
rag_dop_checkpoint.summary: DOP checkpoint configuration, .sim caching, and simulation recovery

rag_dop_python_pattern.description: Complete end-to-end DOP network creation pattern. Source geometry verification, DOP network creation, objects, forces, constraints, solver, output, caching. Master pattern tying all DOP RAG entries together.
rag_dop_python_pattern.summary: Master DOP creation pattern: end-to-end RBD network via Python

rag_dop_common_errors.description: Consolidated DOP error patterns. Frame-1 explosion, fall-through, no-object-to-solve, unexpected reset, freeze requirement, substep instability. Diagnostic functions for each.
rag_dop_common_errors.summary: DOP error diagnostics: explosion, fall-through, missing objects, instability

rag_render_usd_pipeline.description: USD render pipeline hierarchy: RenderSettings → RenderProduct → RenderVar. How to traverse and read these prims. How Karma LOP creates them automatically under /Render.
rag_render_usd_pipeline.summary: USD render pipeline: RenderSettings → RenderProduct → RenderVar hierarchy

rag_render_karma_lop.description: Karma LOP as unified render configuration. Creates RenderSettings + RenderProduct + camera binding. Key parms: resolutionx/y, camera (USD prim path), picture, samplesperpixel. CPU vs XPU differences.
rag_render_karma_lop.summary: Karma LOP unified render configuration: resolution, camera, output, samples

rag_render_output_path.description: Render output path configuration. Set path on BOTH Karma LOP (picture) AND usdrender ROP (outputimage). soho_foreground=1 for synchronous rendering. Output directory creation and verification.
rag_render_output_path.summary: Render output path: Karma LOP picture + ROP outputimage, soho_foreground

rag_render_frame_tokens.description: CRITICAL: Three different frame token systems. Houdini: $F4. husk CLI: <F4>. USD timeSamples: explicit per-frame. Using the wrong format causes silent failures.
rag_render_frame_tokens.summary: Frame token disambiguation: $F4 (Houdini) vs <F4> (husk) vs USD timeSamples

rag_render_branch_convention.description: USD /Render scene graph branch convention. All render configuration under /Render/. renderSettingsPrimPath stage metadata. Multiple render configs. Karma LOP auto-follows convention.
rag_render_branch_convention.summary: USD /Render branch convention and renderSettingsPrimPath metadata

rag_render_karma_namespace.description: Karma property namespaces for per-prim render control. karma:object: (visibility, matte, phantom), karma:light: properties. USD-encoded parameter names. Edit Properties LOP integration.
rag_render_karma_namespace.summary: Karma property namespaces: karma:object: visibility/matte/phantom per-prim control

rag_render_delegate_detection.description: Render delegate detection via Hydra registry. Query available delegates, check AOV support, validate material compatibility per delegate. BRAY_HdKarma vs BRAY_HdKarmaXPU capabilities.
rag_render_delegate_detection.summary: Render delegate detection, capabilities, and material compatibility checking

rag_render_husk_cli.description: husk standalone USD renderer at $HFS/bin/husk. Key flags: --renderer, --frame, --frame-range, --output (<F4> tokens), --res, --pixel-samples. iconvert for EXR-to-JPEG conversion.
rag_render_husk_cli.summary: husk CLI rendering: flags, frame tokens, EXR output, iconvert preview pipeline

solaris_instancing.description: Point Instancer setup in Solaris via the `copytopoints` LOP (H22 canonical rename of the H21 `instancer` LOP; `layout` is now `paintinstances` — emit canonical spellings only): prototype configuration, SOP-to-instancer workflow (transformsourcemode='extsop' + pointsoppath on H22), RBD-to-instancer pipeline, light instancing (reference mode for Karma), variant instancing workaround (Duplicate + Set Variant), Retime Instancer for animated prototypes, SOP attribute mapping (Cd -> inputs:color, primvars array indexing), instanceable flag via Configure Primitive, Karma XPU nesting limits.
solaris_instancing.summary: Solaris instancing: Point Instancer setup (H22: copytopoints, ex-instancer), SOP/RBD workflows, light instancing, variant workaround, animated prototypes, instanceable flag

solaris_variants.description: USD variant creation and selection in Solaris: Add Variant LOP (Begin/End block), Set Variant LOP, variant naming, clean variant setup via chained Grafts, LOD variants, material/look variants, variant precedence in LIVRPS, variant selection in Python, Component Output export with variants, variant composition rules.
solaris_variants.summary: USD variants in Solaris: Add/Set Variant LOPs, clean Graft workflows, LOD/look patterns, LIVRPS precedence, export

solaris_vex_wrangle.description: LOPs Wrangle VEX patterns: setting displayColor (array syntax), xformOp translate/rotate/scale with xformOpOrder, material assignment via usd_setrelationshiptargets, texture path manipulation, cross-stage reads (op: prefix), primpath expressions ({usd_istype}, {s@info:id}), random instance orientations, array attribute editing. Fundamentally different from SOP VEX — no @P/@N/@Cd, everything via usd_setattrib/usd_getattrib.
solaris_vex_wrangle.summary: LOPs VEX wrangle: displayColor arrays, xformOps, material binding, cross-stage reads, primpath selectors, USD attribute functions

solaris_caching_layers.description: Solaris caching and layer management: layer concepts (active layer, layer stack, layer break), save path configuration, Cache LOP file caching, USD ROP per-frame export, Value Clip LOP for animated sequences, Geometry Clip Sequence (H21), VDB sequence looping, UsdConfigure SOP static/dynamic optimization (170MB->30MB), UsdStitch/UsdStitchClips CLI, layered cache strategy (static + animation separation), Scene Graph debugging (blue=static, green=dynamic), flatten and prune, layer muting.
solaris_caching_layers.summary: Solaris caching/layers: layer stack management, Value Clips, Geometry Clip Sequences, static/dynamic optimization, scene graph debugging

aces_color_management.description: Covers setting up an ACES pipeline in Houdini 21 through OpenColorIO: check_ocio_config() verifies the OCIO env var and lists ACEScg/ACES2065-1/sRGB/Raw color spaces via PyOpenColorIO. COLOR_SPACE_RULES maps color textures (albedo, base_color, emission) to srgb_texture and data textures (normal, roughness, metalness, displacement) to Raw, applied to MaterialX mtlximage nodes via the 'colorspace' parm. configure_aces_render_output() keeps the Karma beauty EXR in ACEScg (linear) with no baked display transform, while exr_to_jpeg_aces() uses iconvert to apply the OCIO display transform for previews. Flags common mistakes like applying ACES transforms to data maps, double-converting sRGB (washed-out/oversaturated), and viewing linear EXRs flat/dark without a view transform.
aces_color_management.summary: ACES/OCIO color management pipeline for Houdini 21 (texture color spaces, Karma ACEScg output, display transforms)

camera_sensor_database.description: Provides a CAMERA_SENSORS dictionary mapping production camera bodies (ARRI Alexa 35 / Mini LF, RED V-Raptor X and Komodo-X, Sony Venice 2 and FX6, Blackmagic URSA 12K, Canon C500 II) to their sensor_mm dimensions, max_res, format, color science, and gate_fit. apply_camera_sensor() and create_matched_camera() build a Solaris camera LOP setting horizontalAperture, verticalAperture, focalLength, and fStop; set_camera_sensor_usd() does the same via UsdGeom.Camera on an open USD stage. Helpers calculate_fov (HFOV = 2*atan(hAperture/(2*focal))), calculate_crop_factor, and calculate_equivalent_focal_length cover FOV and full-frame-equivalent math, alongside a GATE_FIT_MODES reference. Key gotcha: camera parms use plain USD names (horizontalAperture, focalLength) directly, NOT the xn__ light-style encoding.
keywords: match render camera to real camera body
camera_sensor_database.summary: Real camera-body sensor database mapped to USD/Solaris camera parameters for accurate FOV matching

copernicus_compositing.description: Builds a Copernicus GPU comp network inside /img using a cop2net with cop:-prefixed nodes: loads beauty and utility AOVs via cop:file, rebuilds beauty by summing light passes (direct/indirect diffuse, specular, emission, sss) through cop:layer_comp set to "add", then chains cop:grade (lift/gamma/gain, saturation), cop:exposure, cop:saturation, cop:ocio_transform (ACEScg to sRGB), and cop:tonemap (aces) before writing EXR via cop:rop_comp. Also covers cop:crypto_extract for selective Cryptomatte grading, cop:denoise with oidn/optix and albedo/normal aux AOVs, plus cop:depth_effects fog, cop:motion_blur (2D from motion vectors), and cop:glow bloom. Includes a GPU VRAM budget table (4K/8K vs AOV count) and the rule to work in linear and apply display transforms only at output. Legacy cop2net syntax here; see copernicus_python_api.md for the modern copnet pattern.
copernicus_compositing.summary: GPU compositing in Copernicus (Houdini 21's COP replacement): AOV beauty rebuild, grade, tonemap, denoise, and cryptomatte via Python.

mpm_production_workflows.description: Covers eight production MPM scenarios from the SideFX H21 Masterclass driven by the MPM solver and mpmobject DOP with presets (rubber, water, metal, clay) and type surface vs solid fill. Core techniques include using the Jp plastic-deformation attribute to detect fracture zones, the close_gaps VEX wrangle blending point-based vs piece-based deformation, and a 100x scale-up workflow that compensates by multiplying timescale and dividing gravity for sub-centimeter sims. Solver tuning covers surfacetension_method (point), minsubsteps/substeps for tension stability, sleepthreshold auto-sleep for snow, compressionhardening for metal tearing, and a two-pass multi-resolution setup (particlesep 0.11 lo-res vs 0.06 hi-res). Also includes narrow-band render-cloud erosion via VDB dilate/smooth/erode, the freeze-deleted-particles pattern for stable point indices during retargeting, and disabling "Assume Unchanging Material Properties" for staggered mid-sim projectile emission.
mpm_production_workflows.summary: Production MPM (Material Point Method) destruction and effects workflows in DOPs

mpm_solver.description: Builds an MPM sim via the SOP chain source_geo -> mpmconfigure -> mpmsource -> mpmcollider -> mpmsolver -> post-sim nodes. mpmconfigure exposes materialpreset (0=Water, 1=Snow, 2=Sand, 3=Soil, 4=Rubber, 5=Metal, 6=Concrete) plus particlesep, stiffness, density, viscosity, and friction; mpmsolver controls minsubsteps/maxsubsteps, materialcondition, gravity, and the assumeunchanging toggle that must be OFF when emitting mixed materials mid-sim. Covers H21 features: surfacetension (point vs grid method, strength, multiphase phaseid), autosleep (velocity threshold, sleepdelay), and per-voxel collider friction/stickiness via createvdbfromattrib. Post-sim nodes mpmsurface (SDF/Density VDB/Polygon Mesh, NPS models), mpmdebrissource, mpmpostfracture (Jp-based reverse fracture), and mpmdeformpieces handle meshing, debris, and destruction.
mpm_solver.summary: Material Point Method (MPM) simulation setup in SOPs for snow, sand, mud, rubber, metal, and concrete

render_farm.description: A Synapse render-farm orchestrator that renders a frame range through a usdrender ROP (soho_foreground=1 for synchronous writes) driving a Karma LOP, then validates each EXR with oiiotool --stats for black_frame, nan_check, clipping, underexposure, and firefly saturation. classify_scene() tags the USD stage and warmup_render_settings() pulls known-good karma_samples/maxpathdepth/pixelfilterclamp from memory; apply_fix() retries by bumping samples or nudging exposure via xn__inputsexposure_vya under the Lighting Law (intensity always 1.0, brightness only via exposure stops). build_tops_render_farm() wires a ropfetch to a localscheduler or hqueuescheduler for distributed rendering, and generate_report() writes a Markdown report with a Windows toast.
render_farm.summary: Local render-farm orchestrator for sequence rendering with per-frame OIIO validation, auto-fix, and PDG/TOPs distribution

temporal_coherence.description: A diagnostic-and-fix workflow for temporal coherence in Karma LOP animation. diagnose_temporal_artifacts() plus a VEX Wrangle snippet audit for missing rest/velocity attributes and adaptive subdivision; configure_karma_denoiser() locks vm_denoiser to one engine (OIDN=1 or OptiX=2) at vm_samples>=64 with albedo/normal aux passes only; configure_motion_blur() sets vm_xformsamples, vm_geosamples, and vm_shutter with optional vm_velblur; configure_volume_stability() pins vm_volumesteprate to 0.25. A rest-position Point Wrangle bakes v@rest = @P so procedural noise stops swimming, and render_validation_range()/compare_frame_luminance() render a low-res 3-to-10 frame window and measure per-pixel luminance delta to quantify flicker before farm dispatch.
temporal_coherence.summary: Fixing frame-to-frame flicker in Karma animation renders — denoiser, motion blur, volume step rate, and rest position.

tops_advanced.description: Covers advanced TOPS/PDG using pythonprocessor generateTasks/cookTask callbacks with item_holder.addWorkItem and setStringAttrib/setIntAttrib for dynamic (non-wedge) work-item generation from upstream JSON, databases, or directory scans. Details feedbackbegin/feedbackend iterative loops with a Python "Stop Condition" expression, plus partitioners (partitionbyattribute, pythonpartitioner, waitforall, partitionbycombination) and configuring local, HQueue, and Deadline schedulers via maxproccount, topscheduler, jobpriority, and env_ attributes. Includes error handling with pdg.CookError and maxretries/errorcondition, checkpoint/resume through pdg_checkpointfile and node.dirtyAll, and full Python-built pipelines chaining filepattern to ropfetch to waitforall to ffmpegencodevideo. Also documents monitoring via getPDGGraphContext, pdg.workItemState, and cookAsynchronous, with a Common Issues section for stuck, failed, or out-of-order work items.
keywords: why did all my work items fail
tops_advanced.summary: Advanced TOPS/PDG production pipeline patterns — dynamic work items, farm schedulers, feedback loops, and checkpoint/resume
